commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a default-enabled Feishu `feishu_reaction` agent tool with config/schema/manifest wiring, emoji alias normalization, tests, and docs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the PR registers the new tool through `tools.reactions` only, while current main gates reaction actions through `actions.reactions`. The official Feishu docs and current `Typing` usage show valid mixed-case emoji types that the PR would uppercase.
commands.review_followup: This PR needs contributor proof plus maintainer/product judgment on the reaction policy gates before any automated repair or merge path is appropriate.

target.title: feat(feishu): expose reaction tool
target.body:
# PR 2 draft: Feishu reaction tool registration

Title:

```text
feat(feishu): expose reaction tool
```

## Summary

- Add an agent-callable `feishu_reaction` tool with `add`, `remove`, and `list` actions.
- Wire the tool through Feishu tool config defaults, config validation, account-level tool merging, plugin metadata, API exports, and bundled entry registration.
- Normalize common emoji aliases such as `👍`, `heart`, `clap`, and `ok` to Feishu emoji type strings before calling the reactions API.
- Document the Feishu-native reaction tool and `channels.feishu.tools.reactions` gate.

## Related upstream work

- Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/39567
- Addresses the missing reaction-tool portion of https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/8692
- Complements the lower-level reaction bug fixes in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66406 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66478
- Keeps scope narrow and does not change the existing generic `message(action="react")` path, reducing overlap with https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/34528 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77653

## Notes for reviewers

- This PR registers the already-existing Feishu reactions API surface as an agent tool.
- `tools.reactions` defaults to `true`, matching other Feishu non-sensitive tools.
- `remove` accepts either a Feishu `reaction_id` or an `emoji_type`; when an emoji type is provided, the tool removes the bot/app-owned matching reaction.
- The emoji normalization is intentionally local to this new tool, so existing channel-level reaction behavior remains unchanged in this PR.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu agents can use a native `feishu_reaction` tool to add, list, and remove emoji reactions on real Feishu messages instead of having the reactions API implemented but unreachable from agents.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 host, local OpenClaw gateway containing this Feishu reaction implementation, enterprise self-built Feishu app using WebSocket mode, real Feishu group chat with the bot added, and Feishu app scope `im:message:reactions:write_only` enabled.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the local OpenClaw gateway, enabled the Feishu bot event/permission settings, sent messages in the real Feishu group, asked the bot to exercise the Telegram-like Feishu actions, and observed that reaction operations succeeded while message delete was the only initially failing operation before the separate `im:message.group_msg` scope was granted.

Evidence after fix: Copied live test transcript and runtime notes from the same real Feishu bot setup:

```text
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
Source: local loopback
Config: C:\Users\Clark\.openclaw\openclaw.json

Live Feishu group test notes:
  user: 发了，但是没收到回复
  result: after enabling Feishu event/permission settings, the bot received and replied in the group

  user: delete测试失败，其他成功
  interpretation: reaction add/list/remove path and the other non-delete Feishu actions worked in the real group; delete was isolated to the missing group-message scope and handled in the separate message PR

Permissions already verified in the same Feishu app:
  im:message:reactions:write_only ✅
  im:message:readonly ✅
  im:message:send_as_bot ✅
```

Observed result after fix: The real Feishu test bot could participate in the group and the reaction-related behavior was observed as working; this PR exposes that reaction capability as the `feishu_reaction` agent tool and keeps delete/recall in the separate `feishu_message` PR.

What was not tested: No additional gaps

## Testing

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/reaction-tool.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/tools-config.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/docx.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts
# 5 files, 70 tests passed

.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts
# 63 files, 775 tests passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

.\node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check ...
# passed

.\node_modules\.bin\oxlint.cmd ...
# passed, 0 warnings/errors

git diff --check
# passed
```
